# UC-19.2.33 · Proxmox Ceph Pool NEARFULL and OSD Space Pressure

## Description

Ceph pools back Proxmox VM disks and ISO libraries. Crossing utilization thresholds triggers backfill throttling and risks write freezes when `nearfull` or `full` ratios are hit cluster-wide.

## Value

Gives storage teams runway to expand OSDs or rebalance PGs before tenant VM writes stall.

## Implementation

Ingest `ceph df detail` JSON every 15 minutes. Compute `stored_pct` from `stored` / `max_avail` per pool. Track `flags` for nearfull/full. Combine with alert on aggregate cluster raw free percent from `ceph osd df`.

## SPL

```spl
index=virtualization sourcetype="proxmox:ceph_pool" earliest=-4h
| where stored_pct>=75 OR match(flags, "(?i)nearfull") OR match(flags, "(?i)backfill_toofull")
| stats latest(stored_pct) as pct_used by pool_name
| sort -pct_used
```

## Visualization

Gauge (pool utilization), Table (pools over threshold), Timechart (pct_used trend).

## References

- [Ceph — Monitoring OSDs and PGs](https://docs.ceph.com/en/latest/rados/operations/monitoring-osd-pg/)
